channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Supreme Leader orders uranium to remain in country
item.description: Ayatollah Khamenei issued a directive that Iran's near-weapons-grade uranium must not be sent abroad, hardening Tehran's stance against a key U.S. demand in peace talks.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President open to direct talks with Trump
item.description: Taiwan's foreign ministry said President Lai Ching-te would welcome a call with President Trump, which would be unprecedented as China claims the self-governing island.

item.title: ⚠️ Russia launches nuclear exercises with Belarus as NATO tensions rise
item.description: Russia deployed nuclear-capable missiles and issued nuclear munitions to Belarus units in major exercises involving 64,000 personnel, amid heightened tensions over Ukraine and Baltic drone activity.

item.title: ✈️ NATO jets scramble as Baltic states detect drones
item.description: Latvia and Lithuania detected drones in their airspace and urged residents to seek shelter while NATO fighter jets scrambled to intercept, the latest security incidents in the Baltics.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Bank of Japan policymaker calls for rate hike citing war-driven inflation
item.description: BOJ board member Junko Koeda said the central bank should raise interest rates as Middle East war price pressures may push inflation above the 2% target, strengthening the case for a June hike.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish court annuls main opposition party congress in blow to CHP
item.description: Turkey's top court invalidated the 2023 congress of the Republican People's Party (CHP), dealing a major setback to the main opposition amid a political crisis.

item.title: 🇲🇾 Malaysia demands TikTok explain AI deepfake of king
item.description: Malaysian authorities summoned TikTok executives after a fake AI-generated account insulted the king, raising concerns about deepfake regulation.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thai princess Bajrakitiyabha's health deteriorates
item.description: The health of Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha has worsened amid an ongoing medical battle, raising concerns about the monarchy's future.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US seeks to expand energy exports to India, says Rubio
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. aims to boost energy exports to India as part of broader strategic cooperation.

item.title: 🇪🇪 NATO jet downs suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
item.description: A NATO fighter shot down a stray Ukrainian drone that entered Estonian airspace, highlighting the spillover risks from the conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Tehran reviewing latest U.S. response, Trump suggests he can wait
item.description: Iran is reviewing the latest U.S. peace proposal as Trump says he's willing to wait for a response amid stalled nuclear negotiations.

item.title: 💰 Global markets wrap-up amid geopolitical tensions
item.description: Global markets reacted to escalating Middle East tensions and nuclear signals from Russia.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Kremlin dismisses report of China training Russian soldiers
item.description: The Kremlin denied reports that China secretly trained Russian soldiers for the Ukraine war, calling them misinformation.

item.title: 🏛️ Inside the unraveling of U.S. diplomacy under Trump
item.description: Donald Trump's threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping U.S. diplomacy, with allies forging new channels to manage decisions driven by personalities.

item.title: 🇯🇵 BOJ's Koeda calls for rate hike as Middle East war fuels inflation
item.description: Bank of Japan board member Junko Koeda said rate hikes should proceed at an appropriate pace as the Middle East conflict pushes inflation risks higher.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany charges two with plotting to kill Jewish leaders for Iran
item.description: German prosecutors charged a Danish and Afghan national with helping Iran plot the killing of Jewish organization leaders in Germany.

item.title: 🇪🇹 Ethiopia accuses Egypt of obstructing Red Sea access bid
item.description: Ethiopia accused Egypt of blocking its efforts to gain Red Sea access amid ongoing tensions over Nile water rights.

item.title: 🇬🇧 BBC: Inside Trump's diplomacy unraveling
item.description: The BBC examines how Trump's approach is reshaping U.S. foreign relations and diplomatic traditions.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Supreme Court rules on Cuba cruise lines case
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court issued a ruling on cruise line operations involving Cuba under Trump administration policies.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel deports 430 foreign flotilla activists
item.description: Israel deported hundreds of foreign activists who attempted to break the blockade, holding one Israeli participant for questioning.

item.title: 🛢️ Chinese supertankers exit Hormuz as tensions appear to thaw
item.description: Two Chinese supertankers joined vessels exiting the Strait of Hormuz as signals suggest a potential de-escalation in tensions.

item.title: ⚔️ Telegraph: Putin's new phase targets Hungarians in Ukraine
item.description: Russia's new military phase in Ukraine appears to target ethnic Hungarian communities, raising concerns about minority populations.

item.title: 💶 Euro zone growth set to slow as Middle East conflict fuels inflation
item.description: The EU warned that euro zone growth will slow in 2026 as the Middle East war drives inflation higher through energy prices.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanese economy projected to contract 7% due to war
item.description: Lebanon's finance minister said the economy will shrink at least 7% due to the Israel-Hezbollah conflict, deepening the country's crisis.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China blocks Pentagon official's Beijing visit over Taiwan arms
item.description: China blocked a planned visit by a U.S. defense official to Beijing in response to a $14 billion arms package for Taiwan.
